entries.ocr_text:
ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
OF THE
COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
"
PASSED AT THE EXTRAORDINARY SESSION OF THE GENERAL
ASSEMBLY, WHICH WAS BEGUN IN THE CITY OF FRANK-
FORT , KENTUCKY , ON MONDAY , APRIL THE TWENTY -THIRD ,
AND ENDED ON FRIDAY , MAY THE ELEVENTH , NINETEEN
HUNDRED FORTY -FIVE .
CHAPTER 1
(H. B. 1 )
AN ACT appropriating money out of the General Expenditure Fund for
the fiscal year ending June 30 , 1945 , for expenses of Sessions of the
General Assembly , to supplement the appropriation heretofore made
for such fiscal year .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
1. There is hereby appropriated out of the funds in the
General Expenditure Fund not otherwise appropriated for
the fiscal year ending June 30 , 1945 , the sum of $ 75,000.00 for
expenses of sessions of the General Assembly, this appropri-
ation to be supplemental and in addition to the appropriation
for such purpose made by Chapter 1 of the Acts of the Second
Extraordinary Session of the 1944 General Assembly .
2. WHEREAS , the appropriation heretofore made for
expenses of legislative sessions for the fiscal year ending
June 30 , 1945 , has proved insufficient for the purpose , and it
is necessary that funds be immediately provided for this Ex-
traordinary Session of the General Assembly , an emergency
is declared to exist and this Act shall take effect from and
after its passage and approval by the Governor .
Passed April 30 , enrolled May 1, and approved May 2 , 1945 .

It declares an emergency and takes effect immediately upon passage and approval.

entries.ocr_text:
2 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
CHAPTER 2
(H. B. 6 )
AN ACT relating to certain contracts , conveyances , rights and liabilities
of infants , to be known as "The Kentucky G. I. Bill of Rights ."
WHEREAS , the General Assembly finds it to be a fact that the approval ,
by federal authorities , of a contract of loan or credit as eligible for
guaranty under federal laws enacted for the benefit of war veterans ,
constitutes satisfactory assurance of the practicability , fairness and
reasonableness of the contract , so as to make it unnecessary that an
infant entering such a contract be given the special protection of law
relating to contracts of infants ; NOW , THEREFORE :
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. When the repayment , by the borrower , of money
advanced or to be advanced under a contract of loan or credit
has , on application of the borrower , been guaranteed in whole
or in part by the Federal Government or any agency thereof ,
under authority of federal law enacted for the benefit of war
veterans , the borrower shall not thereafter be entitled to
avoid the contract on the ground that he was an infant when
the contract was made , nor shall he or his spouse be entitled ,
on the ground of infancy , to avoid any mortgage or other in-
strument of encumbrance executed contemporaneously with
such contract for the purpose of securing the same, nor any
contractual transaction in connection with such contract of
loan or credit pursuant to which the borrower acquired title
to or an interest in the property encumbered to secure the
loan or credit . For the purpose of any litigation seeking to
enforce the contract of loan or credit , or any encumbrance
securing the same ; for the purpose of any contract or con-
veyance executed under a settlement or refinancing plan ap-
proved by the federal authority by which the original guar-
anty was made ; and for the purpose of making and executing
any contract or conveyance necessary to the full realization
of the rights , privileges and benefits intended to be conferred
by the federal law pursuant to which the guaranty was made ,
including contracts necessary for the proper utilization of
the property acquired with the proceeds of such contract of
loan or credit ; the borrower and his spouse shall be deemed
to be of full age .
entries.citation: Acts of the General Assembly of the Commonwealth of Kentucky, 1945, p. 11

classification.title: AN ACT relating to certain contracts, conveyances, rights and liabilities of infants, to be known as "The Kentucky G. I. Bill of Rights."
classification.summary: This act, known as "The Kentucky G. I. Bill of Rights," states that if a loan or credit contract for a war veteran has been guaranteed by the Federal Government, the borrower (even if an infant) cannot avoid the contract or any related mortgage or encumbrance on the grounds of infancy. For such contracts, the borrower and their spouse are deemed to be of full age.
classification.reasoning: The statute addresses contractual rights and liabilities of infants, specifically in the context of federal loan guarantees for war veterans. It does not contain any language that explicitly or implicitly targets or discriminates against any racial group. Therefore, it is not a Jim Crow law. The document type is 'session_laws' as indicated by 'ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY' and 'CHAPTER'.

entries.ocr_text:
CHAPTER 3 3
§ 2. WHEREAS , the Federal Government has pro-
vided benefits for war veterans which , because of existing
Kentucky law, are not available to persons under the age of
twenty -one years , and such persons are in immediate need of
such benefits , an emergency is declared to exist and this Act
shall take effect from and after its approval by the Governor .
Passed and enrolled May 3 , approved May 4 , 1945.
CHAPTER 3
(S. B. 5 )
AN ACT relating to the Issuance and Retirement of Revenue Bonds for
Airports and Airport Facilities , and declaring an emergency to exist .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
Sec . 1. (1 ) As used in this Act :
(a ) " Governmental Unit " means any city of the first ,
second , third , fourth , fifth or sixth class , or any county , or any
such city acting jointly with the county in which the city is
located .
(b) "Airport facilities " includes land , buildings , equip-
ment , runways and other improvements and appurtenances
necessary or proper for the establishment and maintenance
of airports .
( 2 ) Where a city and county are acting jointly any ac-
tion contemplated by this Act to be taken by the legislative
body of the Governmental Unit shall be taken by an ordinance
of the city legislative body and parallel or companion reso-
lution of the fiscal court .
Sec . 2. For the purpose of defraying the cost of acquir-
ing any airport facilities , either by purchase or construction ,
a Governmental Unit may borrow money and issue negotiable
bonds , but only after an ordinance or resolution has been
adopted by the legislative body specifying the proposed
undertaking , the amount of bonds to be issued and the maxi-
mum rate of interest the bonds are to bear . The ordinance
or resolution shall further provide that the airport facilities
are to be acquired pursuant to the provisions of this Act.
Sec . 3. The bonds may be issued to bear interest at a
entries.citation: Acts of the General Assembly of the Commonwealth of Kentucky, 1945, p. 12

classification.title: AN ACT relating to the Issuance and Retirement of Revenue Bonds for Airports and Airport Facilities
classification.summary: This act authorizes governmental units in Kentucky (cities and counties) to borrow money and issue revenue bonds for the purpose of acquiring or constructing airport facilities. It outlines the process for adopting ordinances or resolutions to specify the undertaking, bond amount, and interest rate.
classification.reasoning: The provided statute text details the process for issuing revenue bonds for airport facilities. There is no mention of race, nor any provisions that implicitly or explicitly discriminate based on race. Therefore, it is not a Jim Crow law.

entries.ocr_text:
4 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
rate not to exceed six per cent per annum , payable either an-
nually or semi -annually, and shall be executed in such man-
ner and be payable at such times not exceeding twenty -five
years from date thereof and at such place or places as the leg-
islative body determine .
Sec . 4. Bonds issued pursuant to this Act shall be ne-
gotiable and shall not be subject to taxation . If any officer
whose signature or counter - signature appears on the bonds
or coupons ceases to be such officer before delivery of the
bonds , his signature or counter -signature shall nevertheless
be valid and sufficient for all purposes the same as if he had
remained in office until delivery . The bonds shall be sold in
such manner and upon such terms as the legislative body of
the Governmental Unit deems best , or any contract for the
acquisition of any airport facilities may provide that pay-
ment shall be made in such bonds . In no event shall any
bonds be negotiated on a basis to yield more than six per cent .
The bonds shall be payable solely from the revenue derived
from the airport facilities , and shall not constitute an in-
debtedness of the Governmental Unit within the meaning of
the Constitution . It shall be plainly stated on the face of
each bond that it has been issued under the provisions of this
Act and that it does not constitute an indebtedness of the
Governmental Unit within the meaning of the Constitution .
Sec . 5. All money received from the bonds shall be ap-
plied solely for the acquisition of the airport facilities , and
the necessary expense of preparing , printing and selling said
bonds , or to advance the payment of the interest on the bonds
during the first three years following the date of the bonds .
Sec . 6. If there is any default in the payment of prin-
cipal or interest of any bond , any court having jurisdiction
of the action may appoint a receiver to administer the air-
port facilities on behalf of the Governmental Unit , with
power to charge and collect rates , charges and fees sufficient
to provide for the payment of any bonds or obligations out-
standing against the airport facilities , and for the payment
of operating expenses , and to apply the income and revenue
in conformity with this Act and the ordinance referred to in
Section 2 of this Act .
entries.citation: Acts of the General Assembly of the Commonwealth of Kentucky, 1945, p. 13

classification.title: An Act Relating to the Issuance of Bonds for Airport Facilities
classification.summary: This act authorizes governmental units to issue bonds for the acquisition of airport facilities. It outlines the terms of these bonds, including interest rates, negotiability, tax exemption, and repayment solely from airport revenues. It also details provisions for the appointment of a receiver in case of default.
classification.reasoning: The provided statute text discusses the issuance of bonds for airport facilities. entries.ocr_text:
CHAPTER 3 5
Sec . 7. At or before the issuance of bonds the legisla-
tive body of the Governmental Unit shall , by ordinance or
resolution , set aside and pledge the income and revenue of
the airport facilities into a separate and special fund to be
used and applied in payment of the cost thereof and in the
maintenance , operation and depreciation thereof . The ordi-
nance or resolution shall definitely fix and determine the
amount of revenue necessary to be set apart and applied to the
payment of principal and interest of the bonds , and the pro-
portion of the balance of the income and revenue to be set
aside as a proper and adequate depreciation account , and the
remaining proportion of such balance shall be set aside for the
reasonable and proper operation and maintenance of the air-
port facilities . The rates and fees to be charged for the serv-
ices of the airport facilities shall be fixed and revised from
time to time so as to be sufficient to provide for payment of
interest upon all bonds and to create a sinking fund to pay the
principal thereof when due , and to provide for the operation
and maintenance of the airport facilities and an adequate de-
preciation account . The provision of KRS 183.450 that the
money derived from the use of the airport shall be first ap-
plied to the maintenance of the airport shall not apply in the
case of airport facilities for which revenue bonds have been
issued under this Act .
Sec . 8. If a surplus is accumulated in the operating and
maintenance funds equal to the cost of maintaining and oper-
ating the airport facilities during the remainder of the cal-
endar , operating or fiscal year , and during the succeeding
like year , any excess over such amount may be transferred at
any time by the legislative body of the Governmental Unit to
the depreciation account , to be used for improvements , ex-
tensions or additions to the airport facilities .
Sec . 9. The funds accumulating to the depreciation ac-
count shall be expended in balancing depreciation in the air-
port facilities or in making new constructions , extensions or
additions thereto . Any such accumulations may be invested
as the legislative body of the Governmental Unit may desig-
nate , and if invested the income from such investment shall
be carried into the depreciation account .
entries.citation: Acts of the General Assembly of the Commonwealth of Kentucky, 1945, p. 14

classification.title: An Act relating to airports and aviation
classification.summary: This act outlines the financial management of airport facilities, specifically concerning the issuance of revenue bonds. It details how income and revenue from airport facilities should be set aside into separate funds for bond payments, maintenance, operation, and depreciation. It also specifies how rates and fees should be fixed to ensure sufficient revenue for these purposes and how surplus funds can be transferred to the depreciation account.
classification.reasoning: The provided statute text discusses the financial management and operational aspects of airport facilities, particularly in relation to revenue bonds. It details how funds should be allocated for bond payments, maintenance, operation, and depreciation. There is no mention of race, segregation, or any discriminatory practices within the text. Therefore, it is not a Jim Crow law. The document type is inferred as 'session_laws' because it is presented as a chapter from a legislative session (Gen Assemb 1945.pdf).

6 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
Sec . 10. The Governmental Unit may issue new bonds
to provide funds for the payment of any outstanding bonds ,
in accordance with the procedure prescribed by this Act . The
new bonds shall be secured to the same extent and shall have
the same source of payment as the bonds refunded .
Sec . 11. If the legislative body of the Governmental
Unit finds that the bonds authorized will be insufficient to ac-
complish the purpose desired , additional bonds may be
authorized and issued in the same manner .
Sec . 12. Any Governmental Unit acquiring any airport
facilities pursuant to the provisions of this Act may , at the
time of issuing the bonds for such acquisition , provide for
additional bonds for extensions and permanent improve-
ments to be placed in escrow and to be negotiated from time
to time as proceeds for that purpose may be necessary . Bonds
placed in escrow shall, when negotiated , have equal standing
with the bonds of the same issue .
Sec . 13. This Act shall constitute a method for the ac-
quisition of airport facilities by Governmental Units in ad-
dition or as an alternate to any other method authorized by
statute .
It is intended to authorize the acquisition of additional
facilities for an airport acquired under authority of KRS
183.370 , as well as the original acquisition of land and other
facilities for an airport . Governmental Units shall have all
authority conferred by KRS 183.370 for the acquisition of
airport facilities . Except as otherwise provided in this Act,
the provisions of KRS 183.380 to 183.460 shall apply to the
operation of airport facilities required under this Act .
Sec . 133. The joint city - county air board of a city of
the first class and of the county in which such city is located
is authorized to defray the cost of acquiring any airport fa-
cilities through the issuance of revenue bonds in the same
manner and under the same conditions as are provided in this
Act for Governmental Units , and for that purpose the air
board shall have all of the powers and duties that by this Act
are granted to or imposed upon Governmental Units and the
legislative bodies thereof . The provisions of KRS 183.150 to
entries.citation: Acts of the General Assembly of the Commonwealth of Kentucky, 1945, p. 15

classification.title: ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
classification.summary: This section of the Acts of the General Assembly from 1945 outlines procedures for governmental units to issue bonds for the acquisition and improvement of airport facilities. It details how new bonds can be issued to refund outstanding bonds, authorize additional bonds if initial funding is insufficient, and provide for escrowed bonds for future extensions and improvements. It also clarifies that this act serves as an additional or alternative method for acquiring airport facilities, referencing existing Kentucky Revised Statutes (KRS) related to airport authority and operation. Specifically, it authorizes joint city-county air boards in first-class cities to use revenue bonds for airport facility costs.
classification.reasoning: The provided statute text deals with the financial mechanisms for acquiring and improving airport facilities by governmental units. It discusses bond issuance, refunding, and escrow procedures. There is no mention of race, segregation, or any differential treatment based on race. Therefore, it is not a Jim Crow law. The document type is 'session_laws' as indicated by 'ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY'.

183.220 shall apply in the case of airport facilities so ac-
quired .
Sec . 14. WHEREAS , there is an urgent need for devel-
opment of facilities for transportation of goods and persons

Passed, enrolled and approved May 9 , 1945 .
CHAPTER 4
(H. B. 5 )
AN ACT appropriating money out of the General Expenditure Fund for
the fiscal year ending June 30 , 1945 , to the Division of Public As-
sistance in the Department of Welfare , to supplement the appropria-
tion heretofore made for such fiscal year .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. There is hereby appropriated , to the Division of

classification.title: AN ACT appropriating money out of the General Expenditure Fund for the fiscal year ending June 30, 1945, to the Division of Public Assistance in the Department of Welfare, to supplement the appropriation heretofore made for such fiscal year.
classification.summary: This act appropriates $331,956.82 from the General Expenditure Fund to the Division of Public Assistance in the Department of Welfare for the fiscal year ending June 30, 1945. The funds are intended to supplement existing appropriations for ordinary expenses of operation, cooperation with the Federal Government under the Social Security Act, administration of Old Age Assistance, Child Welfare Program, Aid to Dependent Children, and Aid to the Blind. The act declares an emergency due to insufficient prior appropriations.

8 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
WHEREAS , the Public Assistance Program of the
Commonwealth is in jeopardy and the intended beneficiaries
of such program are in need of immediate relief , NOW ,
THEREFORE , an emergency is declared to exist and this
Act shall take effect from and after its passage and approval
by the Governor .
Passed and enrolled May 3, approved May 4 , 1945 .
CHAPTER 5
(H. R. 2 )
Resolution appropriating additional compensation for officers and em-
ployees of the Senate and House .
WHEREAS , the General Assembly deems the compensation of the offi-
cers and employees of the Senate and the House of Representatives ,
as provided for in Chapter 88 of the Acts of the General Assembly
of 1936 , inadequate for the proper functioning of the business of the
Senate and the House during the Third Extraordinary Session ,
1944-45 ,
Now , Therefore , be it Resolved by the General Assembly
of the Commonwealth of Kentucky :
Sec . 1. That there is hereby appropriated from the Leg-
islative Session Fund as additional compensation for the fol-
lowing officers and employees of the Senate and the House
of Representatives : Assistant Clerk , Enrolling Clerk , Ser-
geant at Arms , Doorkeeper , Cloakroom-keeper , Janitor and
Pages , to that compensation as provided for in Chapter 88
of the Acts of the General Assembly of 1936 , the sum of One
Hundred Dollars ( $ 100.00 ) per diem , effective beginning
with the first day of the Third Extraordinary Session
1944-45 , for each body , for the duration of this Extraordinary
Session .
Sec . 2. That the aforesaid amount shall be prorated
among the aforenamed constitutional officers and employees ,
according to the per diem set forth in Chapter 88 of the Acts
of the General Assembly of 1936 , and shall be by the Depart-
ment of Finance charged to the account of the aforenamed
officers and employees of the Senate and the House of Rep-
resentatives on this basis .
Sec . 3. That the Clerk of the Senate and the Clerk of
the House of Representatives are directed to issue vouchers

CHAPTER 6
(H. R. 3)
A CONCURRENT RESOLUTION for the appointment of a Committee

Kentucky , and the State Penitentiary at Eddyville , Kentucky , setting
forth the powers and duties of such Committee ; and providing funds
for carrying out the functions of the Committee , and declaring an
emergency to exist .
WHEREAS , the attention of the public has been called to certain evi-
dence of maladministration of the State Reformatory at LaGrange ,
Kentucky , and the State Penitentiary at Eddyville , Kentucky , and
WHEREAS , it is in the public interest that the administraion of the State
Reformatory and the State Penitentiary be inquired into in order
that the General Assembly may take such action as may be neces-
sary to correct any unsuitable conditions that might exist and to
insure the proper and efficient administration of the State Reforma-
tory and the State Penitentiary , NOW , THEREFORE ,
Be it Resolved by the House of Representatives, the Sen-
ate Concurring therein:

paint five members of the Senate and the Speaker of the
House of Representatives shall appoint five members of the

entries.ocr_text:
10 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
of the State Reformatory at LaGrange , Kentucky , and the
State Penitentiary at Eddyville , Kentucky . The appoint-
ment shall be made forthwith upon the passage of this reso-
lution . Not more than 3 of the members appointed from the
Senate and not more than 3 of the members appointed from
the House of Representatives shall be members of the same
political party . Immediately upon appointment , the mem-
bers shall organize by the election of a chairman and shall
proceed to the performance of their duties .
Sec . 2. The committee shall make a thorough investi-
gation of the administration of the State Reformatory and
the State Penitentiary and shall generally examine into con-
ditions surrounding the operation and management of the
Reformatory and the State Penitentiary . For such purpose
the Committee shall have the power to summon witnesses and
administer oaths , and shall have free and full access to all
books , files , records and accounts of the State Reformatory
and the State Pententiary and of any state agency . Any cir-
cuit judge shall have authority upon application of the Com-
mittee to punish as for contempt of court any person refusing
to produce evidence or give testimony before the Committee .
Sec . 3. The Committee shall have authority to hire such
skilled and professional assistants , and such clerical assis-
tants as the Committee considers necessary .
Sec . 4. The members of the Committee shall serve with-
out compensation , but shall be paid their actual and necessary
expenses while carrying out the duties of the Committee .
Sec . 5. There is hereby set aside , out of the money ap-
propriated for the expense of legislative sessions for the fis-
cal year ending June 30 , 1945 , for the carrying out of the
functions of the Committee , the sum of $ 10,000.00 . This sum
shall be deemed to have been encumbered and any unused
portion of same shall not lapse at the end of the fiscal year
ending June 30 , 1945 , but shall be available for expenditure
during the subsequent fiscal year . Expenditures out of the
sum so set aside shall be so authorized upon the voucher of
the chairman of the Committee .
Sec . 6. The Committee shall complete its investigation
at as early a date as practicable , and shall report to the Gen-
entries.citation: Acts of the General Assembly of the Commonwealth of Kentucky, 1945, p. 19

Members serve without compensation but are reimbursed for expenses. $10,000 is appropriated for the committee's work.

eral Assembly in session . If the Committee cannot complete

President of The Senate
Clerk-House of Representatives
Vetoed for the reasons stated in the message attached .
May 9th , 1945 .
VETO MESSAGE OF HOUSE RESOLUTION NO . 3.
TO THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY :
I have received House Resolution No. 3 which purports
to provide for the investigation of the administration of the
prisons at LaGrange and Eddyille . I regard this resolution
as invalid , unnecessary and calculated to interfere with the
investigation being conducted at the present time . It violates
three sections of the Constitution . By the express terms of
Section 80 of the Constitution you are prohibited at a special
session from considering any subject not designated in the
proclamation convening it . This section of the Constitution
is mandatory and any act done outside the subjects specified
in the proclamation are absolutely void and of no effect .
There is nothing in the call to authorize an investigation at
the prisons , or elsewhere . The attempt by this Resolution
to make an appropriation is also invalid . The Resolution was
not embraced by the call and it did not receive fifty - one votes
in the House of Representatives on the final roll - call . The
Resolution contains an emergency clause and attempts to ap-
entries.citation: Acts of the General Assembly of the Commonwealth of Kentucky, 1945, p. 20

classification.title: House Resolution No. 3 (Vetoed)
classification.summary: This document contains a veto message from the Governor regarding House Resolution No. 3, which proposed an investigation into the administration of prisons at LaGrange and Eddyville. The Governor deemed the resolution invalid and unnecessary, citing violations of the state constitution regarding subjects permissible during a special session and the resolution's failure to receive sufficient votes. The resolution also contained an emergency clause.
classification.reasoning: The provided text is a veto message concerning a House Resolution. The resolution itself aimed to investigate prison administration. The veto message focuses on procedural and constitutional grounds for rejecting the resolution, not on any racial aspects. Therefore, it is not a Jim Crow law. The document type is 'session_laws' as it pertains to legislative actions. Confidence is high because the content clearly indicates a procedural veto without any racial implications.

entries.ocr_text:
12 : ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
propriate money . By Section 46 of the Constitution any act
or resolution for the appropriation of money , on its final
passage , must receive the votes of a majority of " all the
members elected to each house ." One hundred members
were elected to the House , and this Resolution on its final
passage received but fifty votes . By Section 55 of the Con-
stitution an emergency clause likewise requires the concur-
rence of a majority of the members elected to each house .
The proposed investigation is wholly unnecessary and
improvident . The Welfare Department started an investi- ..
gation immediately upon discovery that forgeries had been
committed by prisoners working in the Reformatory at La-
Grange . This investigation has been proceeding with dili-
gence under competent direction and with complete publicity .
No one has been or will be spared , but every branch of the
prison will be investigated and the evidence taken and pre-
served so as to be available to the General Assembly in due
time . If any evidence of criminal conduct is disclosed , it will
be laid before the Commonwealth Attorneys of the districts.
where the jurisdiction is vested to prosecute and punish .
Every effort is being made , not only to correct evil practices ,
but to formulate a system which will prevent , so far as pos-
sible , evil practices in the future .
We have obtained the services of General Roy Easley as
Director of Corrections , and he has complete charge of the
reorganization of the prisons . His long experience and high
character is sufficient evidence to the public of the sincerity
and earnestness of the purpose to correct any and all evil
conditions at the prisons .
We have also obtained the cooperation of the Federal
Bureau of Prisons and will continue to do so . This investi-
gation must not be interrupted or interfered with by partisan
attempts to pervert and obstruct it . Such an investigation
would be no help . If the legislature had shown a willingness
to cooperate and assist the present investigation it would
have been welcomed , and arrangements would have been
made to have utilized assistance of that kind . But no sort of
cooperation has been offered . If this investigation discloses
the need of legislation there will be time enough to enact it
